rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 3, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS ADRIAN FOX, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR., SCOTT WINKLER'S Motion to Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2 is granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants Class Action Plaintiffs' discovery of the names and addresses and Vin Numbers of putative class members. The Court has weighed balancing the need for discovery with constitutionally protected privacy rights and imposes appropriate third-party privacy protections. (See Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Super. Ct. (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360, 373; Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. (1994) 7Cal. 4th 1.) The privacy safeguard shall be a protective order limiting viewing of the names and addresses to attorney-eyes only. That portion of the Plaintiffs' motion seeking discovery of putative class members' email addresses and/or phone numbers is denied. The scope of the names and addresses of car owners who have not made a claim under warranty is "owners of subject vehicles equipped with power valve engine screws in the intake manifold." Plaintiffs' attempt to expand the definition to include screws "that are not locked tight or may be prone to loosening" is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to offer proof in their moving papers showing that Defendant is aware of which vehicles meet that added definition or that Defendant has that information. Plaintiffs' supplemental filing also does not meet the necessary level of proof. Defendant's objection that the interrogatory is overbroad is sustained to the extent it seeks to include the expanded definition. Special Interrogatory 2 seeking to identify individuals who have filed a complaint shall be answered separately. The Defendant's objection that the information being sought was previously a topic of discovery is overruled. The parties shall "meet and confer" to work out the language of an appropriate protective order regarding the information to be provided in response to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes to have a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 3, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT PHUONG PHAM, TP PHAM, LLC Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This motion appears to be related to a motion to compel discovery scheduled for hearing on December 8, 2014, in Department 302 at 9:30 a.m. In the interest of efficiency and consistent judicial rulings, the hearing on this motion is continued to December 8, 2014 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302, so that it may be heard at the same time as the pending motion to compel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH M. FEE et al VS. ANDREW B. GIANG MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 3, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS ANDREW GIANG, PACIFIC INPATIENT MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint is overruled. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the elder abuse cause of action. (See 3rd Amended Complaint, par. 51). The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The claim survives pursuant to Cal. Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15657 and plaintiff alleges sufficient facts regarding outrageous conduct. The court overrules as to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiff observed her mother drown in what appeared to be her own excrement. No special medical acumen is necessary to understand that injury. (See 3rd Amended Complaint, par. 67; Ochoa v. Super. Ct. (1985), 39 Cal.3d 159, 170-172.) The Court overrules as to causes of action 6, 7, and 8. The fraud claims are alleged with sufficient particularity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH M. FEE et al VS. ANDREW B. GIANG MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 3, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS ANDREW GIANG, PACIFIC INPATIENT MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied in its entirety. CCP 425.13 does not apply to an elder abuse cause of action, plaintiff can recover fees per Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15657, and defendant cites no reason to strike the allegations regarding the Department of Health.
